quoteLast: ,' he observed, 'and he is already gone.
title: Corleone: A Tale of Sicily
author: Crawford, F. Marion (Francis Marion), 1854-1909

quoteFirst: The Chamber was packed and was quivering with excitement when at
quoteLast: , during the preliminary business, Lloyd George, with a red despatch-box in his hand, came into view from behind the Speaker's chair, and passed with alert and nervous steps to the place on the Treasury bench reserved for him between the Prime Minister, Mr. Asquith, and Mr. Churchill.
title: Lloyd George: The Man and His Story
author: Dilnot, Frank, 1875-1946
